title: Query VPC Flow log using Amazon Athena

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the project.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Project Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
On the AWS sign-in page,
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the project.
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the project.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Project Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Project Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Make sure you are in US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Make sure you are in US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to S3 by clicking on the Services menu in the top, then click on S3 in the Storage section.
Navigate to S3 by clicking on the Services menu in the top, then click on S3 in the Storage section.
On the S3 Page, click on Create Bucket and fill in the bucket details.
Bucket name: Enter athena-lab
Note: S3 bucket name is globally unique, choose a name that is available.
Region: Select US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1
Uncheck the option, Block all public access, and check the acknowledge option.
Leave other settings as default.
Click on Create Bucket.
On the S3 Page, click on Create Bucket and fill in the bucket details.
Bucket name: Enter athena-lab
Note: S3 bucket name is globally unique, choose a name that is available.
Bucket name: Enter athena-lab
Note: S3 bucket name is globally unique, choose a name that is available.
Note: S3 bucket name is globally unique, choose a name that is available.
Region: Select US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1
Region: Select US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1
Uncheck the option, Block all public access, and check the acknowledge option.
Uncheck the option, Block all public access, and check the acknowledge option.
Leave other settings as default.
Leave other settings as default.
Click on Create Bucket.
Click on Create Bucket.
The S3 bucket is created.
The S3 bucket is created.
Select the Bucket and click on the Copy ARN Button, save it to notepad. You will need this for future steps.
Select the Bucket and click on the Copy ARN Button, save it to notepad. You will need this for future steps.
Modify the S3 Bucket policy to allow incoming logs from VPC flow logs.
Modify the S3 Bucket policy to allow incoming logs from VPC flow logs.
Click on the Bucket name and switch to the Permissions tab.
Click on the Bucket name and switch to the Permissions tab.
Scroll to the Bucket policy section and click on the Edit button.
Scroll to the Bucket policy section and click on the Edit button.
Paste the below policy. and replace the bucket ARN in the Resource section below and click on Save changes button.
Paste the below policy. and replace the bucket ARN in the Resource section below and click on Save changes button.
Make sure you are in US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Make sure you are in US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to VPC by clicking on the Services menu at the top, then click on VPC in the Networking & Content Delivery section.
Navigate to VPC by clicking on the Services menu at the top, then click on VPC in the Networking & Content Delivery section.
To create a VPC click on Your VPCs the present in the VIRTUAL PRIVATE CLOUD section on the left sidebar.
To create a VPC click on Your VPCs the present in the VIRTUAL PRIVATE CLOUD section on the left sidebar.
Create a new VPC by clicking on the Create VPC.
Select VPC only
Name tag - optional: Enter MyVPC
IPv4 CIDR block: Enter 192.168.0.0/26
IPv6 CIDR block: Select No IPv6 CIDR block
Tenancy: Default
Click on the Create VPC button to create the MyVPC.
Create a new VPC by clicking on the Create VPC.
Select VPC only
Select VPC only
Name tag - optional: Enter MyVPC
Name tag - optional: Enter MyVPC
IPv4 CIDR block: Enter 192.168.0.0/26
IPv4 CIDR block: Enter 192.168.0.0/26
IPv6 CIDR block: Select No IPv6 CIDR block
IPv6 CIDR block: Select No IPv6 CIDR block
Tenancy: Default
Tenancy: Default
Click on the Create VPC button to create the MyVPC.
Click on the Create VPC button to create the MyVPC.
VPC is now created.
VPC is now created.
By default, instances that are launched in a VPC cannot communicate with the Internet. To enable Internet access, an Internet gateway needed to be attached to the VPC.
By default, instances that are launched in a VPC cannot communicate with the Internet. To enable Internet access, an Internet gateway needed to be attached to the VPC.
Click on Internet Gateways from the left menu and click Create Internet Gateway.
Name Tag : Enter MyInternetGateway
Click on Create Internet Gateway.
Click on Internet Gateways from the left menu and click Create Internet Gateway.
Name Tag : Enter MyInternetGateway
Name Tag : Enter MyInternetGateway
Click on Create Internet Gateway.
Click on Create Internet Gateway.
Select the Internet gateway you created from the list.
Click on Actions.
Select Attach to VPC.
Select the Internet gateway you created from the list.
Click on Actions.
Click on Actions.
Select Attach to VPC.
Select Attach to VPC.
Available VPCs: Select MyVPC
Available VPCs: Select MyVPC
And click on the Attach internet gateway button.
And click on the Attach internet gateway button.
The Internet gateway is now attached with MyVPC.
The Internet gateway is now attached with MyVPC.
To create a subnet click on Subnets the present in the VIRTUAL PRIVATE CLOUD section on the left sidebar.
To create a subnet click on Subnets the present in the VIRTUAL PRIVATE CLOUD section on the left sidebar.
Click on the Create Subnet.
Click on the Create Subnet.
In the VPC ID, select the MyVPC.
In the VPC ID, select the MyVPC.
Create the first subnet, you will use this subnet to launch public instances, this subnet will be associated with the main route table of the VPC:
Subnet name: Enter Public subnet
Availability Zone: Select US East (N. Virginia) / us-east-1a
IPV4 CIDR block: Enter 192.168.0.1/27
Create the first subnet, you will use this subnet to launch public instances, this subnet will be associated with the main route table of the VPC:
Subnet name: Enter Public subnet
Subnet name: Enter Public subnet
Availability Zone: Select US East (N. Virginia) / us-east-1a
Availability Zone: Select US East (N. Virginia) / us-east-1a
IPV4 CIDR block: Enter 192.168.0.1/27
IPV4 CIDR block: Enter 192.168.0.1/27
Finally, click on the Create Subnet to create a subnet.
Finally, click on the Create Subnet to create a subnet.
A subnet is now created.
A subnet is now created.
To modify the auto-assign IP settings for the Public subnet, do the following:
Select the Public subnet
Click on the Actions button
Choose Edit subnet settings from the options.
To modify the auto-assign IP settings for the Public subnet, do the following:
Select the Public subnet
Select the Public subnet
Click on the Actions button
Click on the Actions button
Choose Edit subnet settings from the options.
Choose Edit subnet settings from the options.
Check the option Enable auto-assign public IPv4 address under Auto-assign IP settings and click on Save button.
Check the option Enable auto-assign public IPv4 address under Auto-assign IP settings and click on Save button.
Modification is done now.
Modification is done now.
Go to Route Tables from the left menu and click on Create route table button.
Name: Enter PublicRouteTable
VPC: Select MyVPC from the list.
Click on Create route table button.
Go to Route Tables from the left menu and click on Create route table button.
Name: Enter PublicRouteTable
Name: Enter PublicRouteTable
VPC: Select MyVPC from the list.
VPC: Select MyVPC from the list.
Click on Create route table button.
Click on Create route table button.
Select the PublicRouteTable and go to the Subnet Associations tab.
Click on Edit subnet associations.
Select Public Subnet from the list.
Click on Save associations button.
Select the PublicRouteTable and go to the Subnet Associations tab.
Click on Edit subnet associations.
Click on Edit subnet associations.
Select Public Subnet from the list.
Select Public Subnet from the list.
Click on Save associations button.
Click on Save associations button.
Select the PublicRouteTable from the left panel.
Select the PublicRouteTable from the left panel.
On the Edit routes page, Click on the Add route button.
On the Edit routes page, Click on the Add route button.
Add the Destinations as 0.0.0.0/0 and Select the Internet gateway present.
Add the Destinations as 0.0.0.0/0 and Select the Internet gateway present.
Click on the Save changes button.
Click on the Save changes button.
Navigate to the VPC you just created, click on Actions then select Create Flow Log.
Navigate to the VPC you just created, click on Actions then select Create Flow Log.
Enter the name as MyVPCFlowLog,
Enter the name as MyVPCFlowLog,
Select All in filter options and then set the Maximum Aggregation Interval to 1 minute.
Select All in filter options and then set the Maximum Aggregation Interval to 1 minute.
Select as Send to Amazon S3 Bucket as the Destination.
Select as Send to Amazon S3 Bucket as the Destination.
Paste the copied S3 Bucket's ARN in the S3 Bucket ARN field.
Paste the copied S3 Bucket's ARN in the S3 Bucket ARN field.
Log record format: Select AWS default format
Log record format: Select AWS default format
Tags: Keep it as default.
Tags: Keep it as default.
Click on the Create flow log button.
Click on the Create flow log button.
Once the flow logs are created, head back to the AWS VPC service, select it, and click on Flow Logs.
Once the flow logs are created, head back to the AWS VPC service, select it, and click on Flow Logs.
Make sure you are in the N. Virginia(us-east-1) Region.
Make sure you are in the N. Virginia(us-east-1) Region.
Navigate to EC2 by clicking on the Services menu in the top left, then click on EC2 in the Compute section.
Navigate to EC2 by clicking on the Services menu in the top left, then click on EC2 in the Compute section.
Navigate to Instances from the left side menu and click on Launch Instances button.
Navigate to Instances from the left side menu and click on Launch Instances button.
Under the Name and tags section :
Name : MyEC2Instance
Under the Name and tags section :
Name : MyEC2Instance
Name : MyEC2Instance
Under the Application and OS Images (Amazon Machine Image) section :
Select Quick Start tab and Amazon Linux under it
Amazon Machine Image (AMI) : select Amazon Linux 2023 AMI
Under the Application and OS Images (Amazon Machine Image) section :
Select Quick Start tab and Amazon Linux under it
Select Quick Start tab and Amazon Linux under it

Under the Instance Type section :
Instance Type : Select t2.micro
Under the Instance Type section :
Instance Type : Select t2.micro
Instance Type : Select t2.micro

Click on Create new key pair hyperlink
Key pair name: MyEC2FLowLogsKey
Key pair type: RSA
Private key file format: .pem or .ppk
Click on Create key pair and select the created key pair

Click on Create new key pair hyperlink

Under the Network Settings section :
Click on Edit button
VPC : select MyVPC
Subnet : select Public Subnet
Auto-assign public IP: select Enable
Firewall (security groups) : Select Create a new security group
Security group name : Enter FlowLog-SG
Description : Enter Security group for VPC Flow Logs
To add SSH:
Choose Type: SSH
Source: Anywhere (From ALL IP addresses accessible).
For HTTP, click on Add security group rule,
Choose Type: HTTP
Source: Anywhere (From ALL IP addresses accessible).

Keep everything else as default and click on the Launch instance button.
Keep everything else as default and click on the Launch instance button.
Launch Status: Your instance is now launching, Navigate to Instances page from the left menu and wait until the status of the EC2 Instance changes to running.
Launch Status: Your instance is now launching, Navigate to Instances page from the left menu and wait until the status of the EC2 Instance changes to running.
Note down the IPv4 Public IP Address of the EC2 instance. A sample is shown in the screenshot below.
Note down the IPv4 Public IP Address of the EC2 instance. A sample is shown in the screenshot below.
Please follow the steps in SSH into EC2 Instance.
Once you SSH into the instance, install an Apache Server. To install it, follow the below steps. Run these commands one-by-one.
Once you SSH into the instance, install an Apache Server. To install it, follow the below steps. Run these commands one-by-one.
Copy your instance's Public IP, paste it into your browser, and hit enter.
Copy your instance's Public IP, paste it into your browser, and hit enter.
By Running these commands manually and pasting the Public IP in the Browser, we have generated some traffic.
By Running these commands manually and pasting the Public IP in the Browser, we have generated some traffic.
Logs will be stored in S3 Bucket at an Interval of 5 minutes.
Logs will be stored in S3 Bucket at an Interval of 5 minutes.
Refresh the S3 Bucket and go to the folder AWSLogs/<12 Digit Account number>/vpcflowlogs/us-east-1/<Year>/<Month>/<Date>/.
Note: Wait for at least 5 minutes, if you don't see the folder or the logs inside the folders.
Refresh the S3 Bucket and go to the folder AWSLogs/<12 Digit Account number>/vpcflowlogs/us-east-1/<Year>/<Month>/<Date>/.
Note: Wait for at least 5 minutes, if you don't see the folder or the logs inside the folders.
Logs will be present. Click on the Copy S3 URI button.
Logs will be present. Click on the Copy S3 URI button.
To Copy the current location of the log file, click on the Copy S3 URI button.
To Copy the current location of the log file, click on the Copy S3 URI button.
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to Services menu in the top, then search for AWS Glue and click on it.
Navigate to Services menu in the top, then search for AWS Glue and click on it.
From the left side panel under Data Catalog click on Databases.
From the left side panel under Data Catalog click on Databases.
Click on Add Database button.
Click on Add Database button.
Under database details enter database name as : whizdb
Under database details enter database name as : whizdb
Now, click on Create database button.
Now, click on Create database button.
Now expand the left panel and click on Tables, you will be redirected to set tablet properties.
Now expand the left panel and click on Tables, you will be redirected to set tablet properties.
Under table details add :
Under table details add :
Name : whiztable
Name : whiztable
Database : whizdb
Database : whizdb
Inside Data store Include path of your created s3 bucket and leave other thing as default.
Inside Data store Include path of your created s3 bucket and leave other thing as default.
Under Data format select csv, and click on the Next button.
Under Data format select csv, and click on the Next button.
Under choose of click on Edit schema as JSON button.
Under choose of click on Edit schema as JSON button.
Paste the given json code to the editor, and click on Save button.
Paste the given json code to the editor, and click on Save button.
Now, Click on the Next button.
Now, Click on the Next button.
Leave everything as default and click on the Create button.
Leave everything as default and click on the Create button.
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region.
Navigate to Services menu in the top, then search for Athena and click on it.
Navigate to Services menu in the top, then search for Athena and click on it.
Go to the Query Editor.
Go to the Query Editor.
Click on the Query Settings button and then click on Manage button.
Click on the Query Settings button and then click on Manage button.
Now, click on Browse S3 button, select the S3 Bucket created and click on choose button. In this bucket, you have stored the logs and you will run the SQL query.
Now, click on Browse S3 button, select the S3 Bucket created and click on choose button. In this bucket, you have stored the logs and you will run the SQL query.
Location of query result: Enter s3://athena-lab/AWS logs/
Note: This will same as the S3 Bucket name that we created.
Note: If there is any other bucket selected, remove the entry and select the newly created bucket.
Location of query result: Enter s3://athena-lab/AWS logs/
Note: This will same as the S3 Bucket name that we created.
Note: If there is any other bucket selected, remove the entry and select the newly created bucket.
Expected bucket owner: Enter your account ID [Similar to 9287085xxxxx]
Expected bucket owner: Enter your account ID [Similar to 9287085xxxxx]
Now, Click on Save button.
Now, Click on Save button.
Come Back to Query Tab and Click on the + option to get a new and fresh query editor.
Come Back to Query Tab and Click on the + option to get a new and fresh query editor.

Click on the Run button and the output will show the total number of records present in the WhizTable.

Find the total number of columns present in the table.
There is a total of 14 columns present in the table.
There is a total of 14 columns present in the table.
Once the project steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the right side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and displays whether you have completed this project successfully or not.
